title: Merge BSTs to Create Single BST

testcases.input: trees = [[2,1],[3,2,5],[5,4]] Output: [3,2,5,1,null,4] Explanation: In the first operation, pick i=1 and j=0, and merge trees[0] into trees[1]. Delete trees[0], so trees = [[3,2,5,1],[5,4]]. In the second operation, pick i=0 and j=1, and merge trees[1] into trees[0]. Delete trees[1], so trees = [[3,2,5,1,null,4]]. The resulting tree, shown above, is a valid BST, so return its root.
testcases.expectedOutput: [3,2,5,1,null,4] Explanation: In the first operation, pick i=1 and j=0, and merge trees[0] into trees[1]. Delete trees[0], so trees = [[3,2,5,1],[5,4]]. In the second operation, pick i=0 and j=1, and merge trees[1] into trees[0]. Delete trees[1], so trees = [[3,2,5,1,null,4]]. The resulting tree, shown above, is a valid BST, so return its root.

testcases.input: trees = [[5,3,8],[3,2,6]] Output: [] Explanation: Pick i=0 and j=1 and merge trees[1] into trees[0]. Delete trees[1], so trees = [[5,3,8,2,6]]. The resulting tree is shown above. This is the only valid operation that can be performed, but the resulting tree is not a valid BST, so return null.
testcases.expectedOutput: [] Explanation: Pick i=0 and j=1 and merge trees[1] into trees[0]. Delete trees[1], so trees = [[5,3,8,2,6]]. The resulting tree is shown above. This is the only valid operation that can be performed, but the resulting tree is not a valid BST, so return null.

testcases.input: trees = [[5,4],[3]] Output: [] Explanation: It is impossible to perform any operations.
testcases.expectedOutput: [] Explanation: It is impossible to perform any operations.
